seoTitle: Edit Your Invoice Template in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to customize invoice branding, layout, and email defaults in QuickBooks Online using Custom Form Styles.
intro: A generic invoice doesn't do much for your brand. QuickBooks Online's Custom Form Styles tool lets you upload your logo, pick an accent color, control which columns show, and even tweak the automated email that goes out with each invoice. This guide walks through the full customization flow.

steps.heading: Open Custom Form Styles
steps.body: Click the Gear icon, then select Custom Form Styles under the Company column.

steps.heading: Start a new style
steps.body: Click New Style in the upper right of the dashboard, then select Invoice as the form type.

steps.heading: Customize the design
steps.body: On the Design tab, rename your template, upload your business logo, and select an accent color that matches your brand.

steps.heading: Adjust the content layout
steps.body: Switch to the Content tab, click into the header, table, and footer sections to rename fields, show or hide columns, and add remittance details.

steps.heading: Update email settings and save
steps.body: On the Emails tab, edit the default subject and body message sent to customers, then click Done to save your custom template.

faq.question: Can I have multiple invoice templates for different purposes?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create several custom styles and choose which one to apply when creating a specific invoice.

faq.question: Will editing a template affect invoices I've already sent?
faq.answer: No, template changes apply to invoices going forward, not documents that have already been created and sent.

faq.question: How do I set a template as my default?
faq.answer: Within Custom Form Styles you can mark a specific style as the default so it's automatically applied to new invoices.

faq.question: Can I hide the item description or quantity columns?
faq.answer: Yes, the Content tab's table section lets you toggle visibility for columns like item, description, quantity, and rate.
